scope_creep.notes: TRY-2 scope_creep defect RESOLVED in section 5.6 (Where these pillars come from). Line 163 'The UNESCO Recommendation on the Ethics of AI.' is a bare name with the defining appositive stripped. All three satisfy the mention-and-defer form. XAI (line 83) remains a single-sentence named-and-deferred mention. Black box problem is in-scope (5.4 concepts_introduced, grounds the transparency pillar). No concept taught beyond the four-pillars headline.

beginner_readability.notes: Every pillar and technical term (proxy discrimination, black box problem, accountability gap, risk assessment, explainability, discoverability, XAI) defined plainly on first use, analogy-first. No jargon-dump paragraph. Plain register, short sentences, negligible hedging. Zero distinct findings -> no escalation.

verdict: PASS (try 3). All 6 of 6 checks pass. Mention-and-defer form satisfied. Forward-referenced terms: none. Out-of-scope concepts: none. Corpus is cleared to ground downstream artifacts.
